senses.definition: to publish an image, video, text, or other form of content on to the Internet, particularly on social media

examples.x: She decided to post her vacation photos on Instagram to share her travel experiences with friends and family.
He loves to post motivational quotes and thoughts on his LinkedIn profile to inspire his connections.
The blogger plans to post a new recipe video on YouTube every week to engage with her audience.
After the concert, attendees started to post videos of the performances on various social media platforms.

senses.definition: to use social media applications or websites to post or repost a message, image, etc.

examples.x: I'll share the latest news article on my Facebook page.
She shared an inspirational quote on Twitter.
Let's share this exciting event on Instagram.
They often share funny memes on their social media profiles.
It's easy to share interesting articles on LinkedIn.
The new website allows users to share blog posts on various platforms.

senses.definition: to show support for or interest in a post shared on social media, a blog, etc. by tapping a specific button

examples.x: He liked her tweet on Twitter.
I liked your picture on Instagram.
The blog post has been liked by dozens of readers.
Within minutes of posting, her tweet had already been liked by several people.

senses.definition: to express a negative sentiment or lack of approval towards a post, comment, or content by indicating one's dissatisfaction or disagreement with it, often through the use of a dislike button or similar feature provided by the platform

examples.x: She disliked the comment by clicking the thumbs-down button.
After reading the post, he chose to dislike it to show his disagreement.
Many users disliked the video, leaving negative feedback in the comments.
He disliked the article, finding it misleading and inaccurate.
They disliked the post because it didn't align with their views.

senses.definition: to express one's opinion about something or someone

examples.x: After reading the article, she decided to comment on the author's perspective and add her own insights.
During the meeting, participants were invited to comment on the proposed changes to the project plan.
Listeners were encouraged to call in and comment on the radio show, sharing their opinions on the discussed issues.
During the debate, each candidate had the opportunity to comment on their opponent's stance on various issues.
She didn't hesitate to comment on the new policy during the team meeting, expressing her concerns about its potential impact.

senses.definition: to subscribe to a person or organization's account on a social media platform to check everything that they post or publish

examples.x: I follow my favorite bands on Instagram to stay updated on their latest music releases.
We should follow that organization on LinkedIn for updates on job opportunities.
He follows several news outlets on Facebook to stay informed about current events.
If you follow our company's Instagram account, you'll get exclusive sneak peeks of our upcoming products.
I highly recommend following that artist on YouTube.

senses.definition: to stop receiving updates or posts from another user on social media, removing their content from one's feed or timeline

to associate or label someone or something in a post, comment, or photo, typically by using their username or adding relevant keywords, enabling others to easily find or identify them when viewing the content

examples.x: Don't forget to tag me in the photo from last night's party so I can share it with my friends!
I'll tag the author of this article in my tweet to give them credit for their insightful work.
When you post the group picture, make sure to tag everyone so they know it's up!
He's the one who always tags us in those hilarious memes on Facebook.
I always tag my favorite artists in my drawings inspired by their work.

senses.definition: to include or reference another user's username or handle in a post, comment, or message, often by using the "@" symbol followed by their username, drawing their attention to the content or inviting them to join the conversation

examples.x: I want to mention my friend's new business venture on my Instagram story.
She mentioned me in her Facebook post about our recent collaboration.
You should mention the brand you're wearing in your TikTok video for product promotion.
I'm going to mention your username in my Instagram caption to give you credit for the photo.

senses.definition: to send someone a private message on the social media
to respond or react to a post, comment, or message, typically by providing a direct response or engaging in a conversation related to the original content

examples.x: He replied to the tweet, adding further information to the discussion thread.
The YouTuber replied to the viewer's question in the comments section of the video.
They replied to the Instagram story with emojis to show their support for the cause.
The blogger replied to the reader's comment on the blog post, addressing their concerns and questions.
She replied to the LinkedIn message, expressing interest in the job opportunity mentioned.

senses.definition: to repost or share a message that has already been shared by someone else on X social media

examples.x: He retweets articles from reputable sources to provide valuable information to his followers.

senses.definition: to mark or indicate that a post, tweet, or other content is liked, enjoyed, or of interest by selecting a "favorite" or similar button, allowing users to save or bookmark the content for future reference or to show appreciation for the post
to preserve or bookmark a post, image, video, or other content for later viewing or reference, often by selecting a "save" or similar option provided by the platform

examples.x: She saved the interesting article to read during her commute.
You can save this video to your playlist for future enjoyment.
I often save inspirational quotes I find online.
You can save that funny meme to share with friends later.

senses.definition: to prevent someone from contacting or viewing one's activities on social media

examples.x: She blocked her ex-boyfriend on social media to avoid further communication.
He decided to block the spam account that kept sending him unwanted messages.
The user blocked the online harasser to prevent them from seeing their posts.
They blocked the annoying relative who constantly commented negatively on their photos.
The celebrity blocked the persistent fan who crossed boundaries by sending invasive messages.

senses.definition: to remove restrictions or limitations placed on another user's account, allowing them to regain access to one's profile, posts, or interactions
to notify the platform or its administrators about inappropriate, offensive, or abusive content or behavior by using the provided reporting feature or option

examples.x: If you come across a post that violates the community guidelines, please report it for review.
The website has a reporting system in place to enable users to report spam or fraudulent activity.
The online forum relies on its community members to report any instances of inappropriate behavior or offensive language.
The gaming platform encourages players to report any cheating, hacking, or abusive behavior.
It is important to report any instances of impersonation or account hacking to the platform's security team.

senses.definition: to conceal or make content less visible in one's feed or timeline, often by selecting an option or feature that temporarily or permanently removes the content from view

examples.x: I usually hide ads that don’t interest me.
He chose to hide comments from certain users.
The app lets you hide posts from specific accounts.
You can hide suggested posts if they’re irrelevant.
The platform allows you to hide replies on your posts.

senses.definition: to add someone to the list of contacts on social media

examples.x: They will friend anyone who shares similar interests.
The platform allows you to friend people with similar interests.

senses.definition: to post or send something on X social media

examples.x: She tweeted a photo of her vacation to share with her followers.
He tweets daily updates about his work projects to keep his followers informed.
The company tweets promotional offers to attract customers.
They tweeted their opinions on the latest news story to engage in conversation.
The celebrity tweeted a heartfelt message to thank their fans for their support.

senses.definition: (social media) to write someone a private message
to disable or turn off an account or profile on a digital platform or social media network

examples.x: She decided to deactivate her social media profile for a break from online activity.
They deactivated the account due to suspicious login attempts.
To avoid distractions, he deactivated his profile during exam season.
The social media site sent a confirmation email after she deactivated her account.
He deactivated his gaming profile because he no longer enjoyed playing online.

senses.definition: to voluntarily sign up or register for a service or regular updates, indicating the desire to receive ongoing content or notifications from a specific source
to voluntarily remove or cancel one's subscription or connection to a particular user, page, or channel on a social media platform
